key_insight: HVAF-sprayed Tribaloy-400 formed a denser, more protective oxide scale than detonation-sprayed coating, giving better resistance to molten-salt hot corrosion at 500 °C.
engineering_relevance: For concentrated solar power thermal-storage containers, HVAF T-400 coatings appear more suitable than DS for long-duration exposure to nitrate salt environments.
method: Tribaloy-400 was deposited on ASTM A516 steel by HVAF and detonation spraying.
Coated samples were exposed to 60% NaNO3–40% KNO3 molten salt at 500 °C for up to 1000 h.
Phase, SEM, elemental mapping, and mass-change/spallation observations were used to compare corrosion performance.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports stable oxide formation and minimal salt penetration in HVAF coatings, while DS showed deeper ingress, higher mass gain, and more spallation after long exposure.
watch_item: Results are based on a specific nitrate-salt composition and test temperature, so performance may differ under other CSP salts, thermal cycling, or service conditions.
paper_title: Hot Corrosion Behavior of Thermal Sprayed Using HVAF and DS Techniques with Tribaloy-400 Coatings for Solar Energy Storage Container
